message:
bd-1f42.2.2 + bd-1f42.5.1: hardened tool tests + security regression suite

Add 33 hardened tool tests to e2e_tools.rs covering all 7 tools:
- Bash: stderr, CWD, mixed output, timeout, process tree cleanup,
  special chars, pipes, exit codes (9 tests)
- Read: symlinks, unicode, empty files, binary, CRLF (5 tests)
- Write: large files, unicode, deep nested dirs (3 tests)
- Edit: multiline, special chars, whitespace, file boundaries (5 tests)
- Grep: regex patterns, path scoping, match limits (3 tests)
- Find: deep nesting, many files limit (2 tests)
- Ls: dotfiles, sorting, mixed entries, symlink dirs (4 tests)
- Cross-tool roundtrips (2 tests)

Add 18 security abuse-case regression tests to tools_conformance.rs:
- Path traversal: read/write/edit parent dir, absolute path bypass,
  symlink escape, atomic symlink replacement (6 tests)
- Command injection: stdin null, EXIT trap, metacharacters,
  command substitution (4 tests)
- Environment: env inheritance, CWD verification, HOME access (3 tests)
- Unsafe writes: arbitrary dirs, no backup, no temp files,
  dangerous filenames, no copy-on-write (5 tests)

Also adds diagnostic infrastructure (workspace snapshots, timing,
env capture) to tools_conformance.rs e2e test helpers.

All 293 tests pass (142 e2e_tools + 151 tools_conformance). Clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Microtasks/job queue: QuickJS requires the embedder to drain pending jobs (Promises/queueMicrotask) via JS_ExecutePendingJob. This is the foundation of the PiJS deterministic tick model.
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h

- Module resolution: embedder can install a custom module loader (JS_SetModuleLoaderFunc) to control how import/module names resolve (critical for node:* shims and virtual modules).
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h

- Resource limits: QuickJS supports a memory cap (JS_SetMemoryLimit) and an interrupt hook (JS_SetInterruptHandler) suitable for CPU/time budgets (terminate runaway extensions deterministically).
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h
  Ref (overview + timeout mention): https://quickjs-ng.github.io/quickjs-ng/developer-guide/intro.html

- rquickjs maps these controls into Rust-level APIs (Runtime::set_memory_limit, set_max_stack_size, set_gc_threshold, etc.).
  Ref: https://docs.rs/rquickjs/latest/rquickjs/struct.Runtime.html
